channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: This agency is tasked with protecting elections from cyber attacks. If Trump wins, it could be in danger
item.description: Jen Easterly’s confirmation by the Senate in July 2021 to become director of the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) was about as smooth as Senate votes get. With a résumé that includes time in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Max Ufberg, Fast Company

item.title: iOS 18.1 will fix a bug that made it harder to free up iPhone storage
item.description: With many-megapixel cameras and the ability to shoot video at up to 4K, an iPhone’s storage fills up quickly. Sure, ballooning apps and digital downloads like audiobooks and movies also take up space, but they are easy to offload and reload as needed.
item.dc:creator: Sean Captain, Fast Company

item.title: AI is now a crucial tool in Trump’s image-management arsenal
item.description: An image of Donald Trump cosplaying as a football player last weekend set the tone for the final push of the Republican nominee’s campaign. It depicts Trump as a yoked, veiny-arm baller glowering from within a Steelers uniform on the field at Pittsburgh’s Acrisure Stadium.
item.dc:creator: Joe Berkowitz, Fast Company

item.title: DOJ Warns Elon Musk's Super PAC Over $1 Million Giveaways in Swing States
item.description: Justice Department raises concerns over potential violation of federal law
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Everything leaving Netflix in November 2024
item.description: Netflix is preparing to delete another wave of movies in November 2024, but don't panic, this month's list is another short and sweet one.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: New rules for US national security agencies balance AI's promise with need to protect against risks
item.description: New rules from the White House on the use of artificial intelligence by U.S. national security and spy agencies aim to balance the technology’s promise with the need to protect against its risks.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: How to check your heartbeat with ECG app on your Apple Watch
item.description: The ECG app on Apple Watch Series 4 or later and all models of Apple Watch Ultra generates an ECG similar to a single-lead (or Lead I) ECG.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The EU's Top Court Upholds Decision to Annul Intel's Billion-Euro Antitrust Penalty
item.description: Intel wins fresh victory against European Union competition watchdogs as top court confirms lower tribunal's decision to overturn billion-euro antitrust penalty
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Nvidia to power India’s AI factories with tens of thousands of AI chips
item.description: Nvidia on Thursday announced plans to deploy tens of thousands of Hopper GPUs in India to create AI factories and collaborate with Reliance Industries to develop AI infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: A medida que se escala la IA surgen retos de infraestructura
item.description: Las organizaciones que experimentan con la inteligencia artificial (IA) generativa suelen crear cuentas de nivel empresarial con servicios basados en la nube como ChatGPT de OpenAI o Claude de Anthropic, y las primeras pruebas de campo y los beneficios en productividad pueden inspirarles a buscar má
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges
item.description: "A lot of the efforts that we're doing across the government have a plug into the artificial intelligence world," said Andy Kilianski. The post Two new HHS programs to help with complicated health challenges first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Gartner: 13 AI insights for enterprise IT
item.description: Artificial intelligence is an early stage technology and the hype around it is palpable, but IT leaders need to take many challenges into consideration before making major commitments for their enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One Tech Tip: How to prepare your online accounts in case you die
item.description: Most people have accumulated a pile of data on their social media and digital accounts over their lifetimes.
item.dc:creator: Kelvin Chan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Why we get buggy software
item.description: The manufacturing of physical objects is largely understood, mostly because the same thing is being produced over and over. If you are cranking out widgets, the process is orderly, repeatable, and possibly even perfectable.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Commercial space companies now able to churn out satellites
item.description: Increased satellite production is now becoming the standard now in the space hardware industry. The post Commercial space companies now able to churn out satellites first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Eric White, Federal News Network

item.title: ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure musicians sign AI protest letter against 'unlicensed use' of works
item.description: Musicians from ABBA, Radiohead and The Cure have joined actors and authors in signing a protest letter against the mining of their artistry to build artificial intelligence tools.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Big offshore wind project proposed for New York as other sites are evaluated in 3 states
item.description: BRIGANTINE, N.J. (AP) — Offshore wind energy projects in New York, New Jersey and Maryland are moving forward, as federal regulators examine the proposals and opponents escalate their legal challenges to the work.
item.dc:creator: WAYNE PARRY, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The Rise of AI-Powered Work Management: Asana Unveils AI Studio - A Revolutionary Tool for Building Autonomous Agents
item.description: Asana has launched AI Studio, a no-code tool for building generative AI agents that can automate work planning tasks. Discover how this innovation is changing the game for businesses and individuals alike.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tech giants press Congress to codify AI Safety Institute
item.description: A coalition of more than 60 tech companies and industry groups, including Amazon, Anthropic, Google, Meta, Microsoft and OpenAI, urged congressional leaders Tuesday to pass legislation permanently authorizing the U.S. Artificial Intelligence Safety Institute by the end of the year.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk says he’ll fix the government under Trump. His track record paints a different picture
item.description: Elon Musk is making some big promises while campaigning for former President Donald Trump. But his business record calls into question whether he can deliver.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com, The Mercury News

item.title: Beware of fake parking tickets on your windshield with fake fines
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how scammers use fake parking ticket texts and receipts to con victims. Learn how to protect yourself.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Stopping the rot in AI spending
item.description: They say that half the money spent on advertising is wasted, but the problem is figuring out which half. If that’s true, the problem is arguably worse for AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 6 key mobile and IoT/OT attack trend findings
item.description: The role of the CISO continues to expand, driven by the rising number of breaches and cyberattacks like ransomware, as well as SEC requirements for public organizations to disclose material breaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC
